=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (11:14-11:29) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fnUntraced(function*(a) { ... })`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"overloadedArrow\")"

[D2] (12:10-14:5) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

[D3] (21:14-21:42) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fnUntraced(function*(a) { ... })`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"overloadedFunctionExpression\")"

[D4] (22:10-24:5) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

[D5] (31:14-31:34) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fn((a) => { ... })`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"overloadedArrowNoGen\")"

[D6] (32:9-32:11) TS6133: '_a' is declared but its value is never read.
  (no quick fixes)

[D7] (33:9-33:11) TS6133: '_b' is declared but its value is never read.
  (no quick fixes)

[D8] (34:9-34:11) TS6133: '_c' is declared but its value is never read.
  (no quick fixes)

[D9] (35:9-35:11) TS6133: '_d' is declared but its value is never read.
  (no quick fixes)

[D10] (36:9-36:11) TS6133: '_e' is declared but its value is never read.
  (no quick fixes)

[D11] (44:14-44:47) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fn(function(a) { ... })`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen\")"

[D12] (45:9-45:11) TS6133: '_a' is declared but its value is never read.
  (no quick fixes)

[D13] (46:9-46:11) TS6133: '_b' is declared but its value is never read.
  (no quick fixes)

[D14] (47:9-47:11) TS6133: '_c' is declared but its value is never read.
  (no quick fixes)

[D15] (48:9-48:11) TS6133: '_d' is declared but its value is never read.
  (no quick fixes)

  (no quick fixes)

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = Effect.fnUntraced(function* (a: number | string) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any);
});

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = function(a: number | string) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = (a: number | string) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = function(a: number | string) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}


=== [D1] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = Effect.fn(function* (a: number | string) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any);
});

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = function(a: number | string) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = (a: number | string) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = function(a: number | string) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}


=== [D1] Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"overloadedArrow\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = Effect.fn("overloadedArrow")(function* (a: number | string) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any);
});

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = function(a: number | string) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = (a: number | string) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = function(a: number | string) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}


=== [D2] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = (a: number | string) => {
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = function(a: number | string) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = (a: number | string) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = function(a: number | string) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}


=== [D3] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = (a: number | string) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = Effect.fnUntraced(function* (a: number | string) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any);
});

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = (a: number | string) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = function(a: number | string) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}


=== [D3] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = (a: number | string) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = Effect.fn(function* (a: number | string) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any);
});

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = (a: number | string) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = function(a: number | string) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}


=== [D3] Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"overloadedFunctionExpression\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = (a: number | string) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = Effect.fn("overloadedFunctionExpression")(function* (a: number | string) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any);
});

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = (a: number | string) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = function(a: number | string) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}


=== [D4] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = (a: number | string) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = function(a: number | string) {
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = (a: number | string) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = function(a: number | string) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}


=== [D5] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D5] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D5] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = (a: number | string) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = function(a: number | string) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = Effect.fn(function (a: number | string) {
    const _a = 1;
    const _b = 2;
    const _c = 3;
    const _d = 4;
    const _e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(a as any);
});

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = function(a: number | string) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}


=== [D5] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"overloadedArrowNoGen\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = (a: number | string) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = function(a: number | string) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = Effect.fn("overloadedArrowNoGen")(function (a: number | string) {
    const _a = 1;
    const _b = 2;
    const _c = 3;
    const _d = 4;
    const _e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(a as any);
});

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = function(a: number | string) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}


=== [D11] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D11] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D11] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = (a: number | string) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = function(a: number | string) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = (a: number | string) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = Effect.fn(function (a: number | string) {
    const _a = 1;
    const _b = 2;
    const _c = 3;
    const _d = 4;
    const _e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(a as any);
});


=== [D11] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_overloadsOk.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. When assigning to a const
// with an overloaded interface type, the overload signature is preserved
// by the interface, so it's safe to convert to Effect.fn.

interface OverloadedArrow {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrow: OverloadedArrow = (a: number | string) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpression {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpression: OverloadedFunctionExpression = function(a: number | string) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(a as any)
  })
}

interface OverloadedArrowNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedArrowNoGen: OverloadedArrowNoGen = (a: number | string) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a as any)
}

interface OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen {
  (a: number): Effect.Effect<number>
  (a: string): Effect.Effect<string>
}
export const overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen: OverloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen = Effect.fn("overloadedFunctionExpressionNoGen")(function (a: number | string) {
    const _a = 1;
    const _b = 2;
    const _c = 3;
    const _d = 4;
    const _e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(a as any);
});
